title: Bookkeeping and accounting firm
excerpt: My business is a bookkeeping and accounting firm. We do not do year end taxes and do not have a tax season. We process monthly and quarterly bookkeeping, payroll on many different schedules, monthly and quarterly sales t…

posts.author: thequickbooksguy (James Wegener)
posts.cookedHtml:
My business is a bookkeeping and accounting firm. We do not do year end taxes and do not have a tax season. We process monthly and quarterly bookkeeping, payroll on many different schedules, monthly and quarterly sales tax, etc.

We have about 100 client that we process the above reoccurring services for. Its just the same thing month after month, quarter after quarter, etc.

The question is, for my business and the above scenario, what would the basis of a Board be? Would each client be a separate board with that clients services on the board, or would each service be a separate board with each client that has that service on each board?

Also is there a area in Monday that houses a list of clients and client info or would that just be a board as well?

Also any info that could be given on best practices on setting up this type of business on Monday would also be greatly appreciated.

Thanks
James

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @thequickbooksguy

thequickbooksguy:

The question is, for my business and the above scenario, what would the basis of a Board be? Would each client be a separate board with that clients services on the board, or would each service be a separate board with each client that has that service on each board?

Neither is right or wrong exactly… it depends on how you manage your business and divide tasks among your staff. If someone has to click around through 100 boards to do their job, though, that would probably be less than ideal.

It also depends on whether you will be communicating with clients through the platform or if this will only be an internal, backoffice system.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
thequickbooksguy:

Also is there a area in Monday that houses a list of clients and client info or would that just be a board as well?

Meant to also address this - Yes, it is very common to make a CRM style list of clients / contacts as a board and use that as a lookup reference and/or linked column on other boards (Connect Boards column - https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000635139-Connect-boards-column-Previously-Link-to-item-column- )

posts.author: Jakub (Jakub Slusarek)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi James

I am in the same industry, and I have had the same question as you. To be honest, there is no ideal system out there, that has exactly what we need, however i’ve spend 100+ hours finding solution.

If you are interested, we can do a teams and discuss our findings, and maybe make an article for others to use with best practicies

jakub

posts.author: optimizetax (Optimize Tax LLC)
posts.cookedHtml: Great question, James! We run a bookkeeping and accounting firm—Optimize Tax LLC—and use Monday to manage recurring services just like yours. Happy to share what’s worked for us!

posts.author: Martha (Martha)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi James,

For your bookkeeping firm, a practical approach is to create a client information board that houses all client details, contact info, and services they use. Then, have service-specific boards for recurring tasks like monthly bookkeeping, payroll, quarterly sales tax, or catch up bookkeeping services. Each service board can link to the client list so you can see which clients need which service, track deadlines, and maintain organization.

Using templates and automations for recurring tasks will save time and help manage all clients efficiently—almost like having a 24 hour bookkeeper system in place. This structure keeps everything scalable, clear, and easy to navigate.

posts.author: hrmbassociate (HRMB Associates)
posts.cookedHtml:
don’t do a board per client. Best setup is:

  • One Clients board for all client info.

  • Separate service-based boards (bookkeeping, payroll, sales tax), with each client as an item.

  • Link boards and use automations for recurring work.

It scales better and is much easier to manage with 100+ clients
